subagents.result: Confirmed high-confidence payload-shaped rules for ridership, vacancies, subway_fires, and B&T revenue_vehicles; recommended revenue_vehicles normalize to vehicles/count, not riders/ridership.

implemented.rule: Exact ridership metric with transit-agency entity/scope, date/period, and raw million text matching the stored value normalizes to riders/ridership.
Exact vacancies metric with MTA-wide/all-agencies context, date/period, and integer raw count matching the stored value normalizes to vacancies/workforce.
Exact subway_fires metric with subway scope/entity/mode, date/period, and integer raw count matching the stored value normalizes to fires/safety.

Exact revenue_vehicles metric with B&T scope/entity, date/period, and integer raw count matching the stored value normalizes to vehicles/count. This intentionally replaces the earlier context-test expectation that treated the B&T row as riders.

guardrails: No broad metric-name-only fallback was added.
Bare numeric ridership, vacancies, subway_fires, and revenue_vehicles tests remain unresolved unless payload carries the required scope/entity/date/raw-value shape.
next_candidates: Classify remaining date-shaped substantial_completion_date and year-valued other unit-family rows as date/temporal records or defer to representation repair.
Inspect cares_program_count and workforce_development source tables for whether they are participant/program counts or mislabeled categorical dashboard rows.
Keep internal_eeo_complaints_by_basis deferred until the stored value conflict is resolved.
Keep procurement_amount deferred while value is null.
